trans-unit.source: Exercise 2: Analysing Measles Case Data and Population Distribution
trans-unit.target: Exercise 2: Analysing Measles Case Data and Population Distribution

trans-unit.source: Background
trans-unit.target: Background

trans-unit.source: %% MAKE THEM LOOK FOR THE WORLDPOP DATASET ON DEMOGRAPHY AND MAKE THEM CALCULATE THE UNDER 5 POP RASTER
trans-unit.target: %% MAKE THEM LOOK FOR THE WORLDPOP DATASET ON DEMOGRAPHY AND MAKE THEM CALCULATE THE UNDER 5 POP RASTER

trans-unit.source: The Epidemiology Department has shared a line-list of suspected measles cases reported by health districts. Your task in this exercise is to combine this surveillance data with population estimates from WorldPop to identify districts with high measles incidence rates. This will help the response coordination team prioritise vaccination deployments and plan logistics for outreach activities.
trans-unit.target: The Epidemiology Department has shared a line-list of suspected measles cases reported by health districts. Your task in this exercise is to combine this surveillance data with population estimates from WorldPop to identify districts with high measles incidence rates. This will help the response coordination team prioritise vaccination deployments and plan logistics for outreach activities.

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: __In case you did the exercise **Creating an overview map of the health system and vaccination coverage**, you do not need to download this dataset. If you do this exercise without doing the previous exercise, download all datasets provided by HeiGIT [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/modul_5/gis_training_public_health_exercise_2/GIS_Training_Public_Health_exercise_2.zip). and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__
trans-unit.target: __In case you did the exercise **Creating an overview map of the health system and vaccination coverage**, you do not need to download this dataset. If you do this exercise without doing the previous exercise, download all datasets provided by HeiGIT [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/modul_5/gis_training_public_health_exercise_2/GIS_Training_Public_Health_exercise_2.zip). and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__

trans-unit.source: Dataset name
trans-unit.target: Dataset name

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: Source
trans-unit.target: Source

trans-unit.source: Download link / note
trans-unit.target: Download link / note

trans-unit.source: QGIS project created in Exercise 1
trans-unit.target: QGIS project created in Exercise 1

trans-unit.source: [Previous Exercise](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_3/en_module_3_public_health_ex_1.html)
trans-unit.target: [Previous Exercise](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_3/en_module_3_public_health_ex_1.html)

trans-unit.source: Local project folder `/project/`
trans-unit.target: Local project folder `/project/`

trans-unit.source: Chad administrative boundaries (level 2 – districts)
trans-unit.target: Chad administrative boundaries (level 2 – districts)

trans-unit.source: OCHA
trans-unit.target: OCHA

trans-unit.source: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-tcd)
trans-unit.target: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-tcd)

trans-unit.source: 2025 population estimate per grid-cell
trans-unit.target: 2025 population estimate per grid-cell

trans-unit.source: WorldPop
trans-unit.target: WorldPop

trans-unit.source: [WorldPop](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=72895)
trans-unit.target: [WorldPop](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=72895)

trans-unit.source: Reported measles cases by district (line list)
trans-unit.target: Reported measles cases by district (line list)

trans-unit.source: Ministry of Health (MoH) Epidemiology Dept.
trans-unit.target: Ministry of Health (MoH) Epidemiology Dept.

trans-unit.source: Provided for this exercise by HeiGIT
trans-unit.target: Provided for this exercise by HeiGIT

trans-unit.source: Tasks
trans-unit.target: Tasks

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Open the Project and Prepare the Workspace
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Open the Project and Prepare the Workspace

trans-unit.source: Open the QGIS project created in Exercise 1:
trans-unit.target: Open the QGIS project created in Exercise 1:

trans-unit.source: Project → Open… → chad_health_infrastructure.qgz.
trans-unit.target: Project → Open… → chad_health_infrastructure.qgz.

trans-unit.source: Verify that the administrative boundaries and health facility layers load correctly.
trans-unit.target: Verify that the administrative boundaries and health facility layers load correctly.

trans-unit.source: If any layers show an error, use the “Re-link missing layers” dialog to correct their file paths.
trans-unit.target: If any layers show an error, use the “Re-link missing layers” dialog to correct their file paths.

trans-unit.source: Now, lets configure the "Project Home" in the browser panel.
trans-unit.target: Now, lets configure the "Project Home" in the browser panel.

trans-unit.source: In the browser panel on the left, <kbd>right-click</kbd> on `Project Home` → `Set Project Home...` and set the project home folder to the training folder (with the subfolders `/data`, `/project`, etc.). Now you will be able to access all the datasets for this training through the browser.
trans-unit.target: In the browser panel on the left, <kbd>right-click</kbd> on `Project Home` → `Set Project Home...` and set the project home folder to the training folder (with the subfolders `/data`, `/project`, etc.). Now you will be able to access all the datasets for this training through the browser.

trans-unit.source: Task 2: Calculate the Population per District
trans-unit.target: Task 2: Calculate the Population per District

trans-unit.source: In order to calculate the incidence rate per district, we first need to know the population in each district. In many humanitarian contexts, there may be no recent or reliable census data available due to conflict, displacement, or limited national statistical capacity. In such cases, we can use WorldPop population estimates to approximate the population per district. WorldPop produces high-resolution gridded population datasets by combining census data, satellite imagery, land cover information, and statistical modelling to predict population distribution. While these estimates are very useful for planning and epidemiological analysis, it is important to remember that they are modelled estimates, not exact counts, and may carry some uncertainty.
trans-unit.target: In order to calculate the incidence rate per district, we first need to know the population in each district. In many humanitarian contexts, there may be no recent or reliable census data available due to conflict, displacement, or limited national statistical capacity. In such cases, we can use WorldPop population estimates to approximate the population per district. WorldPop produces high-resolution gridded population datasets by combining census data, satellite imagery, land cover information, and statistical modelling to predict population distribution. While these estimates are very useful for planning and epidemiological analysis, it is important to remember that they are modelled estimates, not exact counts, and may carry some uncertainty.

trans-unit.source: Add the WorldPop 2025 raster (`tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif`) via drag-and-drop or: `Layer → Add Layer → Add Raster Layer….`
trans-unit.target: Add the WorldPop 2025 raster (`tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif`) via drag-and-drop or: `Layer → Add Layer → Add Raster Layer….`

trans-unit.source: Take the time to investigate the new layer. Where is the population concentrated? What is the highest or median cell value? How is raster data different to vector data? :::{tip} You can use the Identify Tool ![](/fig/qgis_identify_features.png) to click on the raster and see population estimates per pixel. :::
trans-unit.target: Take the time to investigate the new layer. Where is the population concentrated? What is the highest or median cell value? How is raster data different to vector data? :::{tip} You can use the Identify Tool ![](/fig/qgis_identify_features.png) to click on the raster and see population estimates per pixel. :::

trans-unit.source: We will now calculate the total population for each district using the tool `Zonal Statistics`
trans-unit.target: We will now calculate the total population for each district using the tool `Zonal Statistics`

trans-unit.source: In the __[Processing Toolbox](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_1/en_qgis_start.html#toolbox-toolbars)__, search for "Zonal Statistics" and open the tool.
trans-unit.target: In the __[Processing Toolbox](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_1/en_qgis_start.html#toolbox-toolbars)__, search for "Zonal Statistics" and open the tool.

trans-unit.source: Set the parameters as follows:
trans-unit.target: Set the parameters as follows:

trans-unit.source: Input layer: `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB`
trans-unit.target: Input layer: `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB`

trans-unit.source: Raster layer: `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1`
trans-unit.target: Raster layer: `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1`

trans-unit.source: Raster band: 1 (there will be only one option available)
trans-unit.target: Raster band: 1 (there will be only one option available)

trans-unit.source: Ouput column prefix: `population_`
trans-unit.target: Ouput column prefix: `population_`

trans-unit.source: Statistics to calculate: `Sum`
trans-unit.target: Statistics to calculate: `Sum`

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: The Result will be a new layer called `Zonal Statistics`. This is a temporary layer, indicated by the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_temp_layer.png) on the right of the layer name.
trans-unit.target: The Result will be a new layer called `Zonal Statistics`. This is a temporary layer, indicated by the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_temp_layer.png) on the right of the layer name.

trans-unit.source: Make the layer permanent by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it → `Make permanent` and give it a name like `tcd_pop_2025_adm2`.
trans-unit.target: Make the layer permanent by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it → `Make permanent` and give it a name like `tcd_pop_2025_adm2`.

trans-unit.source: Apply a graduated classification to visualize the district-level population sum results :::
trans-unit.target: Apply a graduated classification to visualize the district-level population sum results :::

trans-unit.source: Task 3: Calculate the Population under 5 per District
trans-unit.target: Task 3: Calculate the Population under 5 per District

trans-unit.source: Measles disproportionately affects young children, especially those under five, who are more likely to develop severe complications and have higher mortality rates. Knowing the under-5 population per district helps estimate how many children are at greatest risk, plan targeted vaccination campaigns, and assess whether health resources are sufficient to protect the most vulnerable age group ([WHO on Measles](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/measles)).
trans-unit.target: Measles disproportionately affects young children, especially those under five, who are more likely to develop severe complications and have higher mortality rates. Knowing the under-5 population per district helps estimate how many children are at greatest risk, plan targeted vaccination campaigns, and assess whether health resources are sufficient to protect the most vulnerable age group ([WHO on Measles](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/measles)).

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Worldpop data in age and sex structures :class: tip WorldPop also provides population estimates by age and sex. This is especially important in our scenario, as we need to know the under-5 population for each district. To find this, go to the Age Structures section and search for Chad.
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Worldpop data in age and sex structures :class: tip WorldPop also provides population estimates by age and sex. This is especially important in our scenario, as we need to know the under-5 population for each district. To find this, go to the Age Structures section and search for Chad.

trans-unit.source: Can you find and download the WorldPop raster containing the population under 5? For a hint look [here](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/listing?id=138). If you scroll down, there is list of the Data Files for the specific age groups. :::
trans-unit.target: Can you find and download the WorldPop raster containing the population under 5? For a hint look [here](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/listing?id=138). If you scroll down, there is list of the Data Files for the specific age groups. :::

trans-unit.source: Add the population under 5 rasters (`tcd_t_00_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif` and `tcd_t_01_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1`) via drag-and-drop or: `Layer → Add Layer → Add Raster Layer….`
trans-unit.target: Add the population under 5 rasters (`tcd_t_00_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif` and `tcd_t_01_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1`) via drag-and-drop or: `Layer → Add Layer → Add Raster Layer….`

trans-unit.source: We will now calculate the total population under 5 for each district using the “Zonal Statistics” tool. Repeat the steps from the previous task. Since the under-5 population is split across two input rasters, you will need to run the same procedure twice.
trans-unit.target: We will now calculate the total population under 5 for each district using the “Zonal Statistics” tool. Repeat the steps from the previous task. Since the under-5 population is split across two input rasters, you will need to run the same procedure twice.

trans-unit.source: Set the parameters as follows:
trans-unit.target: Set the parameters as follows:

trans-unit.source: Input layer: `tcd_pop_2025_adm2` (output from the previous task already containing the total population sum per districts)
trans-unit.target: Input layer: `tcd_pop_2025_adm2` (output from the previous task already containing the total population sum per districts)

trans-unit.source: Raster layer: `tcd_t_00_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1`
trans-unit.target: Raster layer: `tcd_t_00_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1`

trans-unit.source: Raster band: 1 (there will be only one option available)
trans-unit.target: Raster band: 1 (there will be only one option available)

trans-unit.source: Ouput column prefix: `population_under_1_`
trans-unit.target: Ouput column prefix: `population_under_1_`

trans-unit.source: Statistics to calculate: `Sum`
trans-unit.target: Statistics to calculate: `Sum`

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Rename the output in the QGIS Layers panel to `tcd_pop_2025_under_1`
trans-unit.target: Rename the output in the QGIS Layers panel to `tcd_pop_2025_under_1`

trans-unit.source: Repeat this step with the raster `tcd_t_01_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1` as input raster layer and name the output column prefix `population_under_5_`. As Input layer, use the "Zonal Statistics" output from the run for the population under 1 renamed to `tcd_pop_2025_under_1`.
trans-unit.target: Repeat this step with the raster `tcd_t_01_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1` as input raster layer and name the output column prefix `population_under_5_`. As Input layer, use the "Zonal Statistics" output from the run for the population under 1 renamed to `tcd_pop_2025_under_1`.

trans-unit.source: Rename the output in the QGIS Layers panel to `tcd_pop_2025_under_5` and remove the `tcd_pop_2025_under_1` layer ![](/fig/qgis_remove_selected_layer.png) as we don't need it anymore.
trans-unit.target: Rename the output in the QGIS Layers panel to `tcd_pop_2025_under_5` and remove the `tcd_pop_2025_under_1` layer ![](/fig/qgis_remove_selected_layer.png) as we don't need it anymore.

trans-unit.source: Open the field calculator in the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Open the field calculator in the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the attribute table or click on this symbol ![](/fig/qgis_open_attribute_table.png)
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the attribute table or click on this symbol ![](/fig/qgis_open_attribute_table.png)

trans-unit.source: In the tool bar of the attribute table, open the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_open_field_calc_icon.png). The field calculator let's you enter expression to calculate new columns.
trans-unit.target: In the tool bar of the attribute table, open the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_open_field_calc_icon.png). The field calculator let's you enter expression to calculate new columns.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. This is the expression builder.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. This is the expression builder.

trans-unit.source: In the expression builder, we can build and test expressions.
trans-unit.target: In the expression builder, we can build and test expressions.

trans-unit.source: In the middle section, we can open the "Fields and values" header to show the columns of the dataset. Uncollapse it and <kbd>Double-Click</kbd> on `population_under_1_sum` and `population_under_5_sum`. This will add the column to the expression window on the left.
trans-unit.target: In the middle section, we can open the "Fields and values" header to show the columns of the dataset. Uncollapse it and <kbd>Double-Click</kbd> on `population_under_1_sum` and `population_under_5_sum`. This will add the column to the expression window on the left.

trans-unit.source: In the expression window
trans-unit.target: In the expression window

trans-unit.source: Enter the following expression:
trans-unit.target: Enter the following expression:

trans-unit.source: Make sure to give a meaningful "Output field name" such as `total_population_under_5`
trans-unit.target: Make sure to give a meaningful "Output field name" such as `total_population_under_5`

trans-unit.source: Select the correct "Output field type"
trans-unit.target: Select the correct "Output field type"

trans-unit.source: Now remove the fields containing the `population_under_1_sum` and `population_under_5_sum` information as we don't need them anymore. Click on "Delete fields" ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_delete_column_icon.png) and remove these two columns. Save the layer.
trans-unit.target: Now remove the fields containing the `population_under_1_sum` and `population_under_5_sum` information as we don't need them anymore. Click on "Delete fields" ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_delete_column_icon.png) and remove these two columns. Save the layer.

trans-unit.source: Save the enhanced population layer by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`. Select "Geopackage" as the output format and save the layer to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `tcd_pop_2025_under_5`. Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Save the enhanced population layer by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`. Select "Geopackage" as the output format and save the layer to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `tcd_pop_2025_under_5`. Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Task 4: Import and Explore the Measles Cases List
trans-unit.target: Task 4: Import and Explore the Measles Cases List

trans-unit.source: %% Revise this step.
trans-unit.target: %% Revise this step.

trans-unit.source: [Import](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#text-data-import) the `measles_cases_adm2` dataset as a __delimited text layer__ with no geometry.
trans-unit.target: [Import](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#text-data-import) the `measles_cases_adm2` dataset as a __delimited text layer__ with no geometry.

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited Text Layer...`. A new window will open.
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited Text Layer...`. A new window will open.

trans-unit.source: To the right of file name, click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three points and navigate to the file in the `/data/input/`-folder. Click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: To the right of file name, click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three points and navigate to the file in the `/data/input/`-folder. Click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: In the import window, you will see sample data in the sample data field. Take a look at the columns and data available. What kind of data is present in each column? The measles cases don't have any geometry information.
trans-unit.target: In the import window, you will see sample data in the sample data field. Take a look at the columns and data available. What kind of data is present in each column? The measles cases don't have any geometry information.

trans-unit.source: Under `Geometry Definition` select `No geometry (attribute only table)`.
trans-unit.target: Under `Geometry Definition` select `No geometry (attribute only table)`.

trans-unit.source: Check if the sample data displays correctly. Make sure the data type is correct (e.g., cases as integers, not as string).
trans-unit.target: Check if the sample data displays correctly. Make sure the data type is correct (e.g., cases as integers, not as string).

trans-unit.source: Click `Add`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Add`.

trans-unit.source: :::{note} As with other data formats, you can drag-and-drop csv-files onto your map canvas and it will be loaded into your project. __However__, this will lead to mistakes in the data format for each column as it assumes that every column contains text (string) data. You will be unable to perform mathematical or statistical operations with these columns.
trans-unit.target: :::{note} As with other data formats, you can drag-and-drop csv-files onto your map canvas and it will be loaded into your project. __However__, this will lead to mistakes in the data format for each column as it assumes that every column contains text (string) data. You will be unable to perform mathematical or statistical operations with these columns.

trans-unit.source: Make sure to always load csv data via the data source manager and not via the drag-and-drop function.
trans-unit.target: Make sure to always load csv data via the data source manager and not via the drag-and-drop function.

trans-unit.source: Explore the new data file by opening the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Explore the new data file by opening the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the `measles_cases_adm2`-layer and open the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the `measles_cases_adm2`-layer and open the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Take a look at the columns and at how the data is being stored.
trans-unit.target: Take a look at the columns and at how the data is being stored.

trans-unit.source: How could we use this data in our map?
trans-unit.target: How could we use this data in our map?

trans-unit.source: Task 5: Joining the measles cases with our adm2 layer
trans-unit.target: Task 5: Joining the measles cases with our adm2 layer

trans-unit.source: We can join the measles cases with our adm2-layer including the population data (`tcd_pop_2025_under_5`):
trans-unit.target: We can join the measles cases with our adm2-layer including the population data (`tcd_pop_2025_under_5`):

trans-unit.source: In the processing toolbox, open the "Join Attributes by Field value"-tool
trans-unit.target: In the processing toolbox, open the "Join Attributes by Field value"-tool

trans-unit.source: __Input layer:__ `tcd_pop_2025_under_5`
trans-unit.target: __Input layer:__ `tcd_pop_2025_under_5`

trans-unit.source: __Table field:__ `ADM2_FR`
trans-unit.target: __Table field:__ `ADM2_FR`

trans-unit.source: __Input layer 2:__ `measles_cases_adm2`
trans-unit.target: __Input layer 2:__ `measles_cases_adm2`

trans-unit.source: __Table field 2:__ `adm2_name`
trans-unit.target: __Table field 2:__ `adm2_name`

trans-unit.source: Under "Layer 2 fields to copy" select `cases_total`
trans-unit.target: Under "Layer 2 fields to copy" select `cases_total`

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: The result will be a new layer called `Joined layer`. Let's open the attribute table and look at the data.
trans-unit.target: The result will be a new layer called `Joined layer`. Let's open the attribute table and look at the data.

trans-unit.source: If everything is correct, let's make the layer permanent under `tcd_pop_2025_measles_adm2`
trans-unit.target: If everything is correct, let's make the layer permanent under `tcd_pop_2025_measles_adm2`

trans-unit.source: Task 6: Calculating the incidence rate
trans-unit.target: Task 6: Calculating the incidence rate

trans-unit.source: Our district layer now includes the total population, the population under 5 and the total number of measles cases per district. With this information, we can calculate the incidence rate.
trans-unit.target: Our district layer now includes the total population, the population under 5 and the total number of measles cases per district. With this information, we can calculate the incidence rate.

trans-unit.source: Open the field calculator in the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Open the field calculator in the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the attribute table
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the attribute table

trans-unit.source: In the tool bar of the attribute table, open the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_open_field_calc_icon.png).
trans-unit.target: In the tool bar of the attribute table, open the ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_open_field_calc_icon.png).

trans-unit.source: A new window will open with the expression builder.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open with the expression builder.

trans-unit.source: Make sure to give a meaningful "Output field name" such as `measles_incidence_rate`
trans-unit.target: Make sure to give a meaningful "Output field name" such as `measles_incidence_rate`

trans-unit.source: In the expression builder, we can build and test expressions.
trans-unit.target: In the expression builder, we can build and test expressions.

trans-unit.source: In the middle section, we can open the "Fields and values" header to show the columns of the dataset. Uncollapse it and <kbd>Double-Click</kbd> on "population_sum". This will add the column to the expression window on the left.
trans-unit.target: In the middle section, we can open the "Fields and values" header to show the columns of the dataset. Uncollapse it and <kbd>Double-Click</kbd> on "population_sum". This will add the column to the expression window on the left.

trans-unit.source: In the expression window
trans-unit.target: In the expression window

trans-unit.source: Enter the following expression:
trans-unit.target: Enter the following expression:

trans-unit.source: Save the layer and the changes.
trans-unit.target: Save the layer and the changes.

trans-unit.source: Great, we have calculated the incidence rate in our polygon layer. Now, we can create a map displaying the information we gained
trans-unit.target: Great, we have calculated the incidence rate in our polygon layer. Now, we can create a map displaying the information we gained

trans-unit.source: Task 7: Creating map of measles incidence rate
trans-unit.target: Task 7: Creating map of measles incidence rate

trans-unit.source: In this task, we will create a map showing the measles incidence rate by district, helping to visualize where the disease burden is highest. We will also add an overview map displaying the population under five, providing important context for understanding the distribution of vulnerable age groups and interpreting the incidence patterns.
trans-unit.target: In this task, we will create a map showing the measles incidence rate by district, helping to visualize where the disease burden is highest. We will also add an overview map displaying the population under five, providing important context for understanding the distribution of vulnerable age groups and interpreting the incidence patterns.

trans-unit.source: Task 7.1: Symbology
trans-unit.target: Task 7.1: Symbology

trans-unit.source: Symbology measles incidence rate
trans-unit.target: Symbology measles incidence rate

trans-unit.source: Use the `tcd_pop_2025_measles_adm2` layer with the incidence rate from the previous calculation
trans-unit.target: Use the `tcd_pop_2025_measles_adm2` layer with the incidence rate from the previous calculation

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the symbology tab
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the symbology tab

trans-unit.source: Select `Graduated` and `measles_incidence_rate` as the "Value"
trans-unit.target: Select `Graduated` and `measles_incidence_rate` as the "Value"

trans-unit.source: Color ramp could be `Reds`
trans-unit.target: Color ramp could be `Reds`

trans-unit.source: Select Mode `Equal Interval` and 5 Classes
trans-unit.target: Select Mode `Equal Interval` and 5 Classes

trans-unit.source: Symbology population under 5
trans-unit.target: Symbology population under 5

trans-unit.source: Use the `tcd_pop_2025_under_5` layer with the information of population under 5
trans-unit.target: Use the `tcd_pop_2025_under_5` layer with the information of population under 5

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the symbology tab
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the symbology tab

trans-unit.source: Select `Graduated` and `total_population_under_5` as the "Value"
trans-unit.target: Select `Graduated` and `total_population_under_5` as the "Value"

trans-unit.source: Color ramp could be `Turbo`
trans-unit.target: Color ramp could be `Turbo`

trans-unit.source: Select Mode `Equal Count` and 5 Classes
trans-unit.target: Select Mode `Equal Count` and 5 Classes

trans-unit.source: Task 7.2: Print Layout
trans-unit.target: Task 7.2: Print Layout

trans-unit.source: Once you are happy with the symbolization and colors of your data, the next step is to create a print layout. By adding additional information such as a title, data sources, projection, description, etc. you provide your audience with the means to contextualise and evaluate the map and it's content by themselves.
trans-unit.target: Once you are happy with the symbolization and colors of your data, the next step is to create a print layout. By adding additional information such as a title, data sources, projection, description, etc. you provide your audience with the means to contextualise and evaluate the map and it's content by themselves.

trans-unit.source: Open a new print layout and give it a name (e.g. Measles Incidence Chad). A new window will open with a blank canvas and a different set of tools. This is the print layout designer.
trans-unit.target: Open a new print layout and give it a name (e.g. Measles Incidence Chad). A new window will open with a blank canvas and a different set of tools. This is the print layout designer.

trans-unit.source: On the left, you will find a toolbar with tools to add and move items on the print layout canvas.
trans-unit.target: On the left, you will find a toolbar with tools to add and move items on the print layout canvas.

trans-unit.source: On the right you will find a list of items you added to the print layout (it is still empty). Beneath this, you will find a tab called __"Item properties"__. This is where you modify the items on your print layout (e.g. enter the text for a text box or change the font).
trans-unit.target: On the right you will find a list of items you added to the print layout (it is still empty). Beneath this, you will find a tab called __"Item properties"__. This is where you modify the items on your print layout (e.g. enter the text for a text box or change the font).

trans-unit.source: Insert a new map by clicking on ![New Map Icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_insert_map_icon.png) (`Add Map`) on the left toolbar, and drawing a rectangle on the print canvas. [Video](/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.md#adding-a-new-map)
trans-unit.target: Insert a new map by clicking on ![New Map Icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_insert_map_icon.png) (`Add Map`) on the left toolbar, and drawing a rectangle on the print canvas. [Video](/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.md#adding-a-new-map)

trans-unit.source: Move and position the map so that the entire country is visible at a reasonable scale. The first map will be used for the measles incidence rate.
trans-unit.target: Move and position the map so that the entire country is visible at a reasonable scale. The first map will be used for the measles incidence rate.

trans-unit.source: For our example we want to add another map. In this overview map we will display the population under 5.
trans-unit.target: For our example we want to add another map. In this overview map we will display the population under 5.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Lock layers and Lock styles for layers When working with multiple maps in the Print Layout, you need to lock the layers and their styles in the Item Properties under the “Layers” section. This ensures that each map remains unchanged, even if you turn layers on or off in the main QGIS window. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Lock layers and Lock styles for layers When working with multiple maps in the Print Layout, you need to lock the layers and their styles in the Item Properties under the “Layers” section. This ensures that each map remains unchanged, even if you turn layers on or off in the main QGIS window. :::

trans-unit.source: Let's add a title:
trans-unit.target: Let's add a title:

trans-unit.source: Click on ![Add text icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_text.png) (`Add text`)
trans-unit.target: Click on ![Add text icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_text.png) (`Add text`)

trans-unit.source: Drag a rectangle on the canvas.
trans-unit.target: Drag a rectangle on the canvas.

trans-unit.source: In the item properties window on the right, you will find a text box with the text "Lorem ipsum". Here you can enter your map title (e.g. Measles Incidence Rate | Chad).
trans-unit.target: In the item properties window on the right, you will find a text box with the text "Lorem ipsum". Here you can enter your map title (e.g. Measles Incidence Rate | Chad).

trans-unit.source: Adjust the font size: Click on the __Font__ dropdown menu and adjust the font size for a title (25p or more). Adjust the text box if necessary.
trans-unit.target: Adjust the font size: Click on the __Font__ dropdown menu and adjust the font size for a title (25p or more). Adjust the text box if necessary.

trans-unit.source: Let's add a legend:
trans-unit.target: Let's add a legend:

trans-unit.source: Click on ![Add legend icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_legend.png) (`Add legend`).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![Add legend icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_legend.png) (`Add legend`).

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the __Item Properties__ panel on the right.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the __Item Properties__ panel on the right.

trans-unit.source: Scroll down a bit and check turn off `Auto Update` by unchecking the check box. Now you can freely edit every item on the legend
trans-unit.target: Scroll down a bit and check turn off `Auto Update` by unchecking the check box. Now you can freely edit every item on the legend

trans-unit.source: Adjust the legend by removing unnecessary layers (which are not seen on the map) and rename the layer in the legend by clicking on ![Edit Icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_legend_edit.png) (`Edit selected item properties`) below the legend entries.
trans-unit.target: Adjust the legend by removing unnecessary layers (which are not seen on the map) and rename the layer in the legend by clicking on ![Edit Icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_legend_edit.png) (`Edit selected item properties`) below the legend entries.

trans-unit.source: Now, let's add a scale bar:
trans-unit.target: Now, let's add a scale bar:

trans-unit.source: Click on ![Add Scale bar icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_scale_bar.png) (`Add Scale bar`)
trans-unit.target: Click on ![Add Scale bar icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_scale_bar.png) (`Add Scale bar`)

trans-unit.source: Draw a rectangle on the map and position the scale bar on the edge of the map. You can adjust the scale bar units (meters, kilometers, ...), the fixed segment width (50 km, 75 km, 100 km, ...) and the number of segments (to the right).
trans-unit.target: Draw a rectangle on the map and position the scale bar on the edge of the map. You can adjust the scale bar units (meters, kilometers, ...), the fixed segment width (50 km, 75 km, 100 km, ...) and the number of segments (to the right).

trans-unit.source: Let's add a north arrow:
trans-unit.target: Let's add a north arrow:

trans-unit.source: Click on ![Add North Arrow Icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_orientation.png) (`Add North Arrow`).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![Add North Arrow Icon](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_orientation.png) (`Add North Arrow`).

trans-unit.source: Drag a rectangle on the print layout. Adjust the size and location of the north arrow. You can also change the icon in the item properties.
trans-unit.target: Drag a rectangle on the print layout. Adjust the size and location of the north arrow. You can also change the icon in the item properties.

trans-unit.source: Add a text box with additional information, sources, the author (you), and date of creation.
trans-unit.target: Add a text box with additional information, sources, the author (you), and date of creation.

trans-unit.source: When you are happy with your print layout. You can export it as a PDF. You can save it in the project folder under "results".
trans-unit.target: When you are happy with your print layout. You can export it as a PDF. You can save it in the project folder under "results".

trans-unit.source: Once you have exported the map. Look at the PDF and make sure it looks how you intended. Some things might look different in the PDF. If it does not look correct you may need to make some adjustments in the symbology.
trans-unit.target: Once you have exported the map. Look at the PDF and make sure it looks how you intended. Some things might look different in the PDF. If it does not look correct you may need to make some adjustments in the symbology.

trans-unit.source: The finished map could look something like this:
trans-unit.target: The finished map could look something like this:

source.ph: name: Main road network and hospitals in Ghana, Africa

target.ph: name: Main road network and hospitals in Ghana, Africa

trans-unit.source: The main map displays the measles incidence rate, while the secondary map shows the population under five. :::
trans-unit.target: The main map displays the measles incidence rate, while the secondary map shows the population under five. :::
